patches.summary: Token-storage facade routing all auth writes through cfg.SaveTokens (single source of truth) plus one-time migration from legacy ~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/auth.json.
patches.reason: Earlier hand-coded auth flows wrote to a separate auth.json file the templated config/client layer never read; the rest of the CLI couldn't see fresh tokens after login. Migration breadcrumb (auth.json.migrated-YYYYMMDD) keeps the legacy file recoverable for one release.
patches.validated_outcome: 8 storage tests pass; round-trip writes via saveTeslaTokens read back via config.Load with matching access_token, refresh_token, token_expiry.

patches.summary: In-process OAuth2 PKCE void-callback flow: opens Tesla's real login page in the default browser, parses the redirected URL the user pastes back, exchanges the code for refresh+access tokens.
patches.reason: Default `tesla auth login` should work for users without adriankumpf/tesla_auth installed. The void-callback trick (redirect_uri=https://auth.tesla.com/void/callback) avoids a webview or custom-scheme handler; Tesla owns MFA, captcha, and SMS at their own login page.
patches.validated_outcome: 15 PKCE tests pass; verifier length matches RFC 7636 minimum, S256 challenge matches expected base64url, URL parsing tolerates whitespace, quotes, missing code, cancellation envelopes, malformed input.

patches.summary: Refactor `tesla auth login` to branch on flags: default = PKCE browser flow, --paste = stdin token, --refresh-token = direct exchange, --via tesla-auth = subprocess. Flags are mutually exclusive.
patches.reason: Different users have different constraints (interactive vs headless vs scripted); a single command with mode flags keeps the surface clean. Hand-edits to tesla_auth_refresh.go (which is itself a hand-coded file, classified VerdictNovel by regen-merge).

patches.summary: Opt-in fallback: subprocess into adriankumpf/tesla_auth (if installed) and parse stdout for the refresh+access tokens.
patches.reason: Some users prefer the native-window GUI flow tesla_auth ships. Detection via exec.LookPath is non-intrusive; only the explicit --via tesla-auth flag actually subprocesses. Surfaces a one-line stderr hint when tesla_auth is detected so users know the alternate path exists.
patches.validated_outcome: 8 subprocess tests pass; canonical tesla_auth Display output parses correctly, malformed output rejected loudly, --debug log noise interleaving tolerated.

patches.summary: Templated drift in internal/client/client.go: added Client.OnTokenExpired callback field and a 401-retry branch in do() that calls the callback to re-mint the bearer and retries the original request once. Wired by rootFlags.newClient() via makeTeslaAutoRefreshCallback.
patches.reason: Tesla bearers expire after 8 hours; without auto-refresh, every long-running session hits 401 and forces a manual `auth refresh`. The right long-term home is a generator hook (deferred); for v1 the templated drift is contained: one struct field, one if-block in do(), one mutex-guarded closure in a new hand-coded file. TESLA_PP_NO_AUTOREFRESH=1 opts out.
patches.validated_outcome: 6 client-side integration tests pass against an httptest server: retry on 401, no callback bubbles 401, refresh failure bubbles original 401, single-retry cap, 200 no-fire, 403 no-fire.
patches.upstream_issue: Track generator hook in cli-printing-press; today regen-merge does not preserve client.go body drift, so every regen requires re-applying this patch.

patches.summary: Fire migrateLegacyAuthJSON eagerly at cli.Execute() start so v0.1 installs are bridged before any subcommand runs. Silent no-op when nothing to migrate, preserves the stderr-quiet contract for --version/--help/agent-context.
patches.reason: Without eager fire, commands that don't load tokens (e.g. `tesla version`) leave the legacy file untouched and a confused user might still try `auth set-token`. Eager migration removes the disconnect window entirely.
patches.validated_outcome: 4 eager-migrate tests pass plus existing 8 storage tests still pass; covers legacy-absent + no-config silent, config-already-has-tokens silent (legacy preserved), legacy-present-empty-config migrates and renames, legacy-unreadable logs and continues.
patches.upstream_issue: Same client.go-style templated drift on root.go Execute() — call sweep candidate for a generator hook later.

patches.summary: Vercel-ready public-key host template embedded via go:embed plus `tesla auth fleet-template [--dest --gen-key --force]` scaffolder. Generates an EC P256 keypair (private at ~/.tesla/<basename>-private.pem mode 600, public into the template's .well-known path) so the user can `vercel deploy --prod` in one command.
patches.reason: Public-key hosting was the biggest UX cliff in Tesla Fleet API setup. Shipping the Vercel template + key-gen scaffolder in-CLI compresses a multi-day domain side quest into a single command.
patches.validated_outcome: 4 fleet-template tests pass: scaffolds without --gen-key (placeholder PEM preserved), --gen-key writes valid EC P256 and mode-600 private key, refuses non-empty dest without --force, --force overwrites template files but preserves existing private key.

patches.summary: Five new tesla auth subcommands (fleet-setup, fleet-register, fleet-login, fleet-refresh, fleet-status) implementing the partner-app OAuth lifecycle. New [fleet] block in config.toml (FleetClientID, FleetClientSecret, FleetAccessToken, FleetRefreshToken, FleetTokenExpiry, FleetPublicKeyDomain, FleetPrivateKeyPath) with SaveFleetTokens() that preserves top-level iOS-app fields. Localhost callback server pattern ported from snowflake-bypass/fleet-oauth helper. Fixed port 8585 (Tesla OAuth enforces exact-match redirect_uri); --client-secret-file preferred over --client-secret to avoid ps-aux visibility.
patches.reason: Fleet API is the only path that covers DOMAIN_VEHICLE_SECURITY commands (lock/unlock/trunk) on 2021+ vehicles. The 5-subcommand surface walks a fresh user from app registration through user OAuth to a working signed-command token without leaving the CLI.
patches.validated_outcome: 24 fleet-auth tests + 6 config tests pass; covers CSRF state binding, secret-file mode 600 enforcement, port-8585 in-use error, fleet-status no-secret-leak guard (scans stdout+stderr), verify-mode short-circuit across all 5 subs, SaveTokens/SaveFleetTokens cross-clobber preservation (KD3 invariant).
patches.upstream_issue: internal/config/config.go is generator-owned (DO NOT EDIT header); the [fleet] block edit is templated drift. R7 permits this with documentation.

patches.summary: `tesla auth ble-pair --vin <vin>` subcommand that detects tesla-control + tesla-keygen on PATH, runs add-key-request via BLE, prompts for the NFC tap, and polls session-info for the resulting key handle. Idempotent: if the key is already enrolled at start, reports the existing handle and exits zero without prompting.
patches.reason: BLE pairing is the second-biggest UX cliff after public-key hosting. The CLI wrapping the tesla-control subprocess turns a 5-step recipe into a single command.
patches.validated_outcome: 10 ble-pair tests pass (8 scenarios + 2 helper unit tests): happy path, tesla-control missing prints install recipe with exit 2, BLE handshake failure, NFC tap timeout, already-enrolled idempotency, invalid --role, verify-mode short-circuit, missing private key with hint.

patches.summary: `tesla auth export` and `tesla auth import` for transporting Fleet credentials + signing key to a remote agent host (e.g. cloud Mac mini). Passphrase-encrypted tar.gz bundle: magic header + Argon2id salt + AES-256-GCM. Passphrase read via term.ReadPassword (no echo, no flag, no env unless explicit --no-prompt opt-in for CI). Manifest schema versioning with major/minor refusal/warning policy.
patches.reason: Multi-machine deployments (laptop -> Mac mini) need a secure transport for the [fleet] block + private key file. Re-doing OAuth + key enrollment on every machine doesn't scale. The encrypted bundle is the canonical path; brokers (Teslemetry/Tessie) are the alternative if the user accepts key surrender.
patches.validated_outcome: 25 export/import tests pass (11 export + 14 import): roundtrip with all includes, partial-include preservation, refuse-without-force on existing non-expired Fleet token, wrong-passphrase generic decryption-failed, corruption detection via GCM auth tag, schema_version mismatch policy, no-passphrase-in-bundle security scan, no-private-key-bytes-in-bundle security scan, verify-mode + dry-run short-circuit, full lifecycle on two ephemeral home dirs.

patches.summary: `tesla relay {start,stop,status,doctor}` subcommand tree managing the external lotharbach tesla-http-proxy as a detached subprocess (POSIX Setsid). Generates one-off TLS cert at ~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/relay-cert.pem on first start. PID/log/state files under ~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/. Windows graceful-degrade: prints unsupported-platform JSON and exits zero. Doctor runs synthetic vehicle_data round-trip and surfaces the known VCSEC-unsupported limitation.
patches.reason: Hermes is the free path for infotainment commands (charge/climate/honk). The subprocess lifecycle (PID file, log redirect, port allocation, TLS cert) is non-trivial enough that having the CLI manage it is materially better than a 10-step README.
patches.validated_outcome: 25 relay tests + 8 helper unit tests pass; cross-builds clean on linux/darwin/windows; full SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL escalation path tested with 5s grace; idempotent start/stop; install-recipe-on-missing-binary; port-in-use --port hint; subprocess early-exit cleanup; doctor VCSEC warning; verify-mode short-circuit across all 4 subs.

patches.summary: `tesla command <name> [--vehicle ...] [--via=auto|fleet|hermes|ble] [--send]` unified router. Path picker in internal/cli/tesla_signed_paths.go is a pure function (CommandClass, vehicleClass, fleetReady, hermesRunning, viaOverride) -> (path, reason). KD6 cost-honest default: Hermes-first for infotainment, Fleet for VCSEC. KD5 side-effect rule: prints intent without --send, fires only with --send. Token written to mode-600 temp file under ~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/tmp/ (mode 700 dir), deleted in defer, stale-tmp sweep helper exposed.
patches.reason: Without a unified router, the user has to know which path supports which command on which vehicle. The router carries that complexity for them and surfaces it via --via overrides + clear errors when a chosen path can't deliver a chosen command (e.g. --via=hermes unlock).
patches.validated_outcome: 53 tests pass (20 path-picker matrix rows + 15 classifyCommand + 14 command-router integration + 4 BLE recipe edge cases): default-print without --send, Fleet send with token-file shape assertion (mode 600 under private tmp), Hermes send via local relay with bearer header assertion, --via=fleet without creds errors, --via=hermes for VCSEC errors, vehicle resolution by name/VIN-suffix/substring/ambiguous, REST-friendly hint, BLE recipe surfacing, verify-mode short-circuit.

patches.summary: Doctor gains fleet_auth + relay + signed_command_paths sections via the existing OK/INFO/WARN/FAIL dispatch table. Reachability adds additive recommended_via and available_paths fields (existing v3 classification enum preserved exactly). agent-context schema bumped 3 -> 4 with three new Fleet env vars and a SignedCommandPaths top-level block (fleet{available,audience,scopes,expiry}, hermes{running,port}, per_vehicle[]). manifest.json user_config gains tesla_fleet_token + tesla_fleet_client_id + tesla_fleet_client_secret keys (object-map shape preserved, all sensitive=true required=false).
patches.reason: Discovery surfaces are the agent-readable contract. Without these updates, an MCP agent looking at agent-context wouldn't know the new Fleet/Hermes paths exist on this install. Schema bump signals the additive change; v3 fields remain at the same paths so v3 readers continue to work.
patches.validated_outcome: 15 tests pass (6 agent-context + 6 reachability + 3 signed-paths reused from U4): schema_version=4, all 3 Fleet env vars in envVars list, SignedCommandPaths well-formed with and without Fleet creds, no-token-literal-leak guard (scans JSON output for access_token/refresh_token/client_secret/client_id byte sequences), v3 backcompat shape preserved with omitempty on new reachability fields.
patches.upstream_issue: doctor.go, tesla_reachability.go, agent_context.go all carry generator DO NOT EDIT headers; the edits are templated drift. R7 permits this with documentation. Future generator hook should make these extension points.

patches.summary: Mechanical scrub of workspace identifiers from source, docs, and manifest files before first publish. Replaced workspace VINs with placeholders across source examples. Scrubbed absolute home paths from polish-state files. Verified the published tree has no workspace identifiers.
patches.reason: R6: no real PII in any file that lands in the publish repo. Vehicle names Snowflake and Stella are explicitly accepted by the author for cookbook examples; everything else is workspace-specific.
patches.validated_outcome: Full test suite (8 packages) still passes after the scrub. Grep for real VINs, real Vercel host, real partner-app UUIDs, real client-secret prefix, real coordinates, real email, and workspace-absolute home paths returns zero matches across .go, .md, .json, .toml, .yaml files.
